run_environment: cowork-sandbox (isolated Linux shell, egress via allowlisted proxy)

pre_kickoff_guard.result: PASS (guard satisfied - run was well before kickoff; failure below is unrelated to timing)

elo_capture_attempts.error: urlopen error Tunnel connection failed: 403 Forbidden
urlopen error Tunnel connection failed: 403 Forbidden

diagnosis: This isolated run's shell sandbox reaches the internet through an allowlisted egress proxy. https://www.eloratings.net is not on that allowlist (confirmed non-transient across two attempts ~90s apart; a control request to https://example.com was also proxy-blocked with the same header, indicating a restrictive default allowlist rather than a site-specific outage). capture_elo_evidence.py therefore could not perform its mandated direct_http_response_body capture (urlopen against OFFICIAL_ELO_SOURCE with HTTP 200 + exact final-URL check). No TSV or receipt file was created by either attempt (the script's own cleanup path correctly removed nothing, since creation never began).
explicitly_not_done: Did NOT fetch World.tsv content via any alternate tool (e.g. general-purpose web fetch) and write it into an evidence file, because that would fabricate a capture_method=direct_http_response_body receipt for bytes that were not actually captured by the sanctioned direct-HTTP path. This is precisely the forgery risk AUTOMATION_RUNBOOK.md's 'Artifact trust boundary' section warns about.

Did NOT proceed to context template / market / weather evidence / predict_jul11.py finalize steps, since the fail-closed contract requires stopping the entire official prediction path on Elo capture failure, not just skipping the Elo module.
Did NOT substitute estimated Elo ratings or the previously known values (Spain 2190 / Argentina 2177 / France 2163 / England 2097 from prior sessions' evidence) as a stand-in for a fresh capture.
Did NOT create or overwrite any evidence/sf101_*.final.json artifact.
recommended_action: Retry this capture from an environment with network access to www.eloratings.net (e.g. the user's local machine, or a session where that domain is allowlisted) before 2026-07-14T19:00:00Z kickoff. Roughly 2h50m of runway remained at the time of this report (16:10 UTC). If no successful capture occurs before kickoff, sf101 has no official artifact and must be recorded as a missed finalization, consistent with the July 11 incident precedent in AUTOMATION_RUNBOOK.md.
